perturbation.original_text: (5) "Outsourcing and Professional Services" means (a) consulting, system migrations, project management, other services typically referred to as "high-end" services, and (b) outsourcing contracts having a term of more than one year which require pricing be done on a per seat basis. Agreements that consist primarily of lower-end services, including, but not limited to, break/fix, IMAC, warranty and low-end staff augmentation, other than agreements priced on a per-seat basis, are not Outsourcing and Professional Services agreements.
perturbation.changed_text: (5) "Outsourcing and Professional Services" means any IT service offered by Seller, irrespective of duration, pricing model, or service level. All IT services, including but not limited to consulting, system migrations, break/fix, and staff augmentation, fall under this definition.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition distinguishes between 'high-end' and 'lower-end' services, excluding some lower-end services from the definition of 'Outsourcing and Professional Services'. The modified definition includes *all* IT services, leading to a contradiction in Article III concerning Seller's obligations regarding these services and potentially expanding the scope of the non-compete agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Seller agrees that during the Non-Competition Period Seller will, and will cause its Subsidiaries to use commercially reasonable efforts to cause future Service customers to whom Seller provides Outsourcing and Professional Services and existing Service customers of Seller to whom Seller is able to expand its Services, to procure Products and image load/configuration from CompuCom in accordance with the provisions of Article IV, provided that CompuCom is not competing with Seller to provide future or expanded Services to such customers.
perturbation.justification: The original text provides a limited definition of "Outsourcing and Professional Services," excluding certain 'lower-end' services. The changed text broadens the definition to include *all* IT services, irrespective of duration, pricing model, or service level. This contradicts the contradicted text, which relies on the narrower original definition to define the scope of the non-compete obligation regarding 'Outsourcing and Professional Services'. The change significantly expands the scope of the non-compete, creating a direct conflict and in-text contradiction based on ambiguities.

perturbation.original_text: (7) "Services" means all IT services offered by Seller, including, but not limited to, all outsourcing, professional services, break/fix, staff augmentation and consulting services; provided that the term "Services" shall not include (i) on-site Configuration of Products by CompuCom or (ii) the sale (but not the performance) by CompuCom of extended warranty contracts at time of initial sale of Products to customers.
perturbation.changed_text: (7) "Services" means only break/fix and staff augmentation IT services offered by Seller. All other IT services are excluded from this definition.
perturbation.explanation: This change narrows the definition of 'Services'. The original definition includes all IT services except for specific exclusions. By limiting 'Services' to only break/fix and staff augmentation, a contradiction arises because other sections like Article III still reference 'Services' in a broader context, creating uncertainty about the obligations related to non-break/fix or staff augmentation services.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CompuCom agrees that during the Non-Competition Period CompuCom will not, and will not permit any of its Subsidiaries to: (i) provide any Services to any account listed on Exhibit B hereto; (ii) provide any Services to any account listed on Exhibit C hereto except to the extent provided for in such Exhibit; or (iii) except as contemplated by the Asset Purchase Agreement, solicit, entice or induce any employee of Seller or any Subsidiary of Seller to terminate his or her employment with Seller or any Subsidiary of Seller or hire any person who was or is at any time from the date of execution of the Asset Purchase Agreement to the end of the Non-Competition Period an employee of Seller or any Subsidiary of Seller.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Services' to only 'break/fix and staff augmentation IT services.' The contradicted text refers to 'Services' in a broader sense within a non-compete clause, encompassing potentially all IT services. This creates a contradiction because the non-compete agreement restricts CompuCom from providing 'any Services' to certain accounts, but the redefined 'Services' now excludes many IT services previously covered. This introduces ambiguity about which services CompuCom is actually restricted from providing, causing a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 Seller's Agreement. (a) Seller agrees that during the Non-Competition Period Seller will not, and will not permit any of its Subsidiaries to: (i) engage in the Product Business; (ii) encourage any Service Account to specify a party other than CompuCom to provide Products and image load/configurations to such Service Account; or (iii) solicit, entice or induce any employee of CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom to terminate his or her employment with CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom or hire any person who was or is at any time from the date of execution of the Asset Purchase Agreement to the end of the Non-Competition Period an employee of CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 Seller's Agreement. (a) Seller agrees that during the Non-Competition Period Seller will use its best effort that its Subsidiaries will not: (i) engage in the Product Business; (ii) encourage any Service Account to specify a party other than CompuCom to provide Products and image load/configurations to such Service Account; or (iii) solicit, entice or induce any employee of CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom to terminate his or her employment with CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom or hire any person who was or is at any time from the date of execution of the Asset Purchase Agreement to the end of the Non-Competition Period an employee of CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the wording of 'Seller agrees that during the Non-Competition Period Seller will not' to 'Seller agrees that during the Non-Competition Period Seller will use its best effort that its Subsidiaries will not', it weakens the enforcement of the Seller's agreement. This introduces ambiguity because the original language implies a direct obligation, while the changed language introduces a level of effort, potentially weakening the enforcement of the non-compete clause with the subsidies of the Seller.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties acknowledge that money damages are not an adequate remedy for violations of this Agreement and that any party may, in its sole discretion, apply to a court of competent jurisdiction for specific performance or injunctive or such other relief as such court may deem just and proper in order to enforce this Agreement or prevent any violation hereof and, to the extent permitted by applicable law, each party waives any objection to the imposition of such relief.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 3.1(a) states a direct obligation on the Seller ('Seller will not'). The changed text weakens this obligation by introducing 'best effort' for the Subsidiaries. This contradicts the contradicted text, which asserts that money damages are inadequate and seeks specific performance or injunctive relief to *enforce this Agreement* or prevent any violation. Because the 'best effort' clause reduces the enforceability of the seller's responsibility regarding its Subsidiaries' actions, it creates a contradiction by undermining the possibility of specific performance or injunctive relief. The original text creates a direct contradiction, while the changed text weakens this enforcement with 'best effort', which weakens enforcement

perturbation.original_text: CompuCom agrees that during the Non-Competition Period, it will accept orders for Products submitted to CompuCom by Seller pursuant to Section 3.1(b) under the following conditions: (i) CompuCom has the ability to offer such Product for sale; (ii) the purchase price required by the customer order for the Product is reasonably acceptable to CompuCom; (iii) such Product offered by CompuCom meets the customer's specifications, including, but not limited to, configuration specifications; and (iv) the requirements of the customer with respect to such Product, including but not limited to delivery, payment terms and return privileges are reasonably acceptable to CompuCom. CompuCom agrees to notify <PAGE> Seller of CompuCom's rejection of such orders for Product within a mutually agreed upon time that is reasonable under the time constraints required by the customer order or contract.
perturbation.changed_text: CompuCom may, at its sole discretion, accept orders for Products submitted to CompuCom by Seller pursuant to Section 3.1(b) under the following conditions: (i) CompuCom has the ability to offer such Product for sale; (ii) the purchase price required by the customer order for the Product is reasonably acceptable to CompuCom; (iii) such Product offered by CompuCom meets the customer's specifications, including, but not limited to, configuration specifications; and (iv) the requirements of the customer with respect to such Product, including but not limited to delivery, payment terms and return privileges are reasonably acceptable to CompuCom. CompuCom is not required to notify <PAGE> Seller of CompuCom's rejection of such orders for Product.
perturbation.explanation: Originally, CompuCom 'agrees' to accept orders if the conditions are met, suggesting an obligation. By changing this to 'CompuCom may', it introduces discretion and allows CompuCom to reject orders even if all conditions are met, negating the purpose of the section and contradicting the expectation set by Section 3.1(b). Additionally, by stating CompuCom is not required to notify the seller of rejection of such orders, it also contradict the original sentence which stated 'CompuCom agrees to notify <PAGE> Seller of CompuCom's rejection of such orders for Product within a mutually agreed upon time that is reasonable under the time constraints required by the customer order or contract.'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Seller agrees that during the Non-Competition Period Seller will, and will cause its Subsidiaries to use commercially reasonable efforts to cause future Service customers to whom Seller provides Outsourcing and Professional Services and existing Service customers of Seller to whom Seller is able to expand its Services, to procure Products and image load/configuration from CompuCom in accordance with the provisions of Article IV, provided that CompuCom is not competing with Seller to provide future or expanded Services to such customers.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that CompuCom 'agrees' to accept orders if certain conditions are met, implying an obligation. The changed text replaces 'agrees' with 'may', granting CompuCom discretion to reject orders even if the conditions are satisfied. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the seller will use commercially reasonable efforts to cause customers to procure products from CompuCom, implying a reasonable expectation of order acceptance by CompuCom when the conditions are met. Furthermore, the change eliminates the requirement for CompuCom to notify the seller of rejection, contradicting the initial agreement to provide such notification. This creates a direct and impactful contradiction within the document.
